claim: Evidence verification confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch already fully supports research/fetch style evidence with external scope separation. No assumptions presented as facts, all evidence paths are usable, and no missing data remains.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both independently verified VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines four scopes: project, external, user, unknown.
evidence.why: Both agents cited same primary source for scope taxonomy, confirming no assumption masquerading as fact.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' proves blocking behavior for external-only evidence.
evidence.why: Test evidence independently verified by both agents proves the acceptance criteria is already satisfied.

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' proves URL-to-path enforcement.
evidence.why: Cross-checker independently verified this test, confirming external URLs cannot be mislabeled as project facts.

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states external evidence cannot prove existing-project facts.
evidence.why: Both agents independently read and cited the same doctrine documentation, confirming it is a fact not an assumption.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function enforces project evidence requirements.
evidence.why: Implementation logic independently verified by both explorer and cross-checker confirms the behavior exists in code.

result.notes: Verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker independently gathered 12 evidence items from code, tests, and docs. All evidence has proper scope declarations, exact paths/lines, appropriate sources, and clear explanations. No assumptions are presented as facts. No missing data remains. No conflicts between agents. The acceptance criteria is already satisfied: WebFetch/WebSearch are supported for external evidence with scope=external, and project facts require project-scoped path evidence. PRD is ready to proceed.
